published_prior_art.cite: Source-label invariance is standard in ICA/cumulant decompositions; no cited work states this particular axis-normalized direction-preservation lemma.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id addresses minimal cumulant order for direct-effect identification under general confounding, not opposite-arrow separation for a fixed bivariate LvLiNGAM.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorems 4.4–4.7, gives an order-2m+3 cumulant-matrix orientation criterion; it does not establish full-fiber separation from T_{2m+2}.
ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 treats a one-latent higher-cumulant direction setting, not the arbitrary-m full opposite-arrow compatibility locus.

per_theorem_calls.label: Open-ended Question 3: minimal real information order

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id notes the distinction between a failed rank witness and an information-order lower bound, but gives no bivariate real opposite-arrow twin family.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction supplies a sufficient orientation criterion, not a Euclidean-open real reverse-twin family matching all cumulants through 2m+1.
SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 uses higher-cumulant rank conditions for multivariate discovery, not a semialgebraic atlas of bivariate feasible opposite-arrow fibers.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / flagship order-frontier questions
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 (ICML 2025), the closest missed finite-cumulant LvLiNGAM result: it invokes Schkoda et al.'s two-observed-variable finite-root theorem and must be distinguished explicitly from the proposed opposite-arrow and minimal-order frontier.

novelty_flags.label: related_work and oeq:real-exceptional-atlas gap
novelty_flags.one_line: Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability proves lack of uniform finite-sample statistical decidability under latent confounding; it does not establish that a real separation restriction, much less this proposed compatibility locus, is necessary.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand / lem:admissible-swaps-preserve-direction
soundness_flags.one_line: D(P) is not a functional of P on the declared model class: opposite-arrow representations may coexist, and gamma=0 or delta=0 is allowed although the target excludes a no-direct-edge value; restrict the domain to separated nonzero-edge SCMs or make D structural rather than law-indexed.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:generic-exceptional-locus
soundness_flags.one_line: E_m is a set of cumulant vectors, but the question says it gives exactly generic parameter points; state the required preimages Phi^b^{-1}(E_m) intersected with Theta^{b,circ} before claiming an exact parameter-level characterization.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 to related work and state precisely why its finite-root recovery under a specified graph does not decide full opposite-arrow compatibility or the 2m+2 frontier.
Redefine the target on a separated structural-model domain, explicitly impose nonzero direct effects, and make the selector the observed-law functional only on that domain.

Work out m=1 and m=2 real compactly-supported source families, including the truncated-moment feasibility inequalities, to make the proposed lower-order twin route empirically and algebraically testable.

recommended_next_step: Revise the target/domain and parameter-versus-image locus statements, then repair the literature positioning before advancing this field-tier kernel to Stage 0.
